Name: The Great Sumerian Deluge
Effect: Put this aside at the start of the game. The first time there are 8 or more humans in play, the flood happens at the end of that turn. The Flood: Banish all humans.
Anecdote: 'The noise of mankind has become too much. I am disturbed by their uproar. Sleep no longer comes to me.' — Enlil, before the council

Name: Craftsmen of the Ark
Anecdote: Gold, silver, his kin, and the craftsmen — all went aboard. Their hands had built the ship; now the ship would carry them.

Name: Flies
Effect: If a being left the world this turn, you may play this for free.
Anecdote: They come where something has ended. They are never far behind.

Name: Slave
Effect: While on top: You may banish this to play your next artefact this turn for [2] less.
Anecdote: His hands know the weight of stone and brick. He has built the city. He will not be remembered in it.

Name: Shepherd
Effect: On enter: The next human you play costs [1] less.
Anecdote: Where he walks, others follow. He does not ask them to.

Name: Farmer
Effect: On enter: Play another 1 cost human for free.
Anecdote: The field is never worked alone. One calls to his neighbor, and his neighbor comes.

Name: Fisherman
Effect: On enter: Draw two humans from your underworld.
Anecdote: He casts his net into the dark water and pulls out what the deep sees fit to return.

Name: Sacrificer at the Altar
Effect: On enter: If the flood would happen at the end of this turn, it happens at the end of your next turn instead.
Anecdote: The gods are not easily turned. But they can be made to wait.

Name: Weeping Mother Goddess
Effect: On enter: Choose one of your humans with a cost of [2] or less and return it to its owner's hand.
Anecdote: 'How could I have agreed to destroy my own children in the council of gods?' She would not be consoled.

Name: Shipbuilder
Effect: While on top: Artifacts you play cost [1] less.
Anecdote: He has never asked what the ship is for. He asks only whether the joints will hold.

Name: Citizen of Shruppak
Anecdote: Shruppak was a city that knew the gods. Its streets were full, and its walls were old. It did not think the world could end.

Name: Elders of Shuruppak
Effect: While on top: Double the power of all your humans here.
Anecdote: They have seen five kings come and go. When they speak, even the young men are quiet.

Name: Atrahasis, Flood Survivor
Anecdote: Ea leaned close to the wall of reeds and spoke just loud enough for one man to hear. That was all it took.

Name: Enlil, Storm God
Effect: On enter: If there are two or more humans here, you may unleash the flood at this location.
Anecdote: He did not hate them. He simply could not sleep.

Name: The Ark
Effect: Costs [1] less for each of your humans. Indestructible. On enter: Choose a location. While on top: Your humans on chosen location are protected from the flood.
Anecdote: Six decks, seven compartments, nine sections. Pitch sealed every seam. Inside: gold, silver, all living things, his kin, the craftsmen, the cattle of the field. The rain began at dawn.
